title: Patriots reportedly miss out on Maxx Crosby trade
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Maxx Crosby to be traded from Las Vegas Raiders to Baltimore Ravens for two first-round picks.
Crosby was interested in joining New England Patriots and Dallas Cowboys, per Dianna Russini.
Patriots have cap space for trades, missing Crosby doesn't halt potential moves, pursuing Eagles' A.J. Brown.

title: Report: Celtics center Nikola Vucevic to miss one month with broken finger
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Nikola Vucevic fractured his right ring finger in Friday's game and is expected to miss one month.
Vucevic sustained the injury during a post-up against the Mavericks and left after playing two minutes.
His absence impacts Celtics' front-court depth, with Jayson Tatum and Amari Williams adjusting back into the lineup.

title: Watch: Jayson Tatum receives thunderous ovation in first game back with Celtics
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jayson Tatum returned to the Celtics' lineup against the Mavericks after recovering from a ruptured Achilles tendon.
Tatum received a warm ovation as he was the last player introduced before the game at TD Garden.
In his return, Tatum played six minutes initially, scoring five points with a dunk and a three-pointer.

title: Report: Patriots to trade C Garrett Bradbury to Bears
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Patriots to trade center Garrett Bradbury to the Bears for a fifth-round draft pick.
The Bears seek a new center after Drew Dalman's retirement; Bradbury, 30, started every game for Patriots in 2025.
Bradbury's trade frees $5.7 million in cap space, potentially allowing Patriots significant free agency spending.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'Hamnet', a speculative film about Shakespeare's son, streams on Peacock.
'It Was Just an Accident', critiquing Iran's regime, streams on Hulu.
'Arrested Development' leaves Netflix on March 15, after over a decade.

title: What Joe Mazzulla said about Jayson Tatum’s return to Celtics starting lineup
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jayson Tatum made his season debut for the Celtics against the Mavericks after a long rehab process.
Coach Joe Mazzulla praised Tatum's work ethic and communication during his recovery, citing gratitude and perspective.
Tatum replaced Baylor Scheierman in the starting lineup with no announced minutes restriction from Coach Mazzulla.

title: Bruins trade for former first-round pick Lukas Reichel
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Boston Bruins acquired Lukas Reichel from Vancouver for a 2026 sixth-round pick.
Reichel, drafted 17th overall in 2020, has 22 goals in 188 NHL games.
Reichel will start with the Providence Bruins to potentially boost his NHL return.

title: 2 Boston restaurants to pay  $400K for failure to pay service fees to staff, AGO alleges
author: Katelyn Umholtz
bullets: Two Boston restaurants, Carrie Nation Cocktail Club and The Dubliner, are paying $422,093 over mislabeled service fees.
From May 2023 to June 2024, 84 service employees did not receive a 3% service fee meant for them.
East Coast Tavern Group claimed a point-of-sale system error misrepresented a 'hospitality' fee as a 'service' fee.

title: Patriots reportedly had this trade offer for A.J. Brown rejected by Eagles
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Patriots reportedly offered Eagles a first and third-round draft pick for A.J. Brown, which was rejected.
Eagles seek a first-round pick with a second-round sweetener for A.J. Brown, per NFL insider Mike Garafolo.
NFL's legal tampering period begins Mar. 9 as trade talks for A.J. Brown expected to conclude this weekend.

title: Connecticut woman running 2026 Boston Marathon after Alzheimer’s strikes her family again
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Colleen Graham from Norwalk, Connecticut, will run the 2026 Boston Marathon in honor of her father diagnosed with Alzheimer's.
Graham aims to raise $30,000 for Alzheimer's research while participating in the marathon.
Her father, a Boston sports fan and former marathon runner, inspired her running journey as she tackles the 26.2-mile race.
